text:
More than $3.25 trillion in trading volume later, @PythNetwork is proving that great markets are built on great data.

It’s not just securing crypto markets anymore.

It’s becoming the pricing layer for everything that trades onchain.

Every month, billions of dollars in trading volume rely on Pyth - and RWA perpetuals are becoming an increasingly important part of that growth.

In May alone, protocols powered by Pyth processed $130B in trading volume, including $110B from RWA perpetuals.

As exchanges expand into commodities, equities, ETFs, and indices, price data becomes the infrastructure that decides which markets can actually scale.

Fast, accurate, first-party price data.

Without it, these markets simply don’t exist.

The future of onchain finance won’t be built by the biggest exchange.

It’ll be built on the most reliable data.

13 Things Everyone Should Know Before Supporting @risextrade 

1) They celebrate $1B cumulative volume... while a regular perp aggregator reached the same milestone in a single week, without an MM.

2) They compare themselves to Hyperliquid... while Hyperliquid compares itself to Binance.

3) They sold a private Kaito community round by teasing a Q4 2025 TGE... it's now the end of Q2 2026.

4) They hired a CIS Community Lead... who never even joined Discord to say hello.

5) They post pictures of food... while their community is starving for updates.

6) They ask the community for feedback... then leave a 👍 and change nothing.

7) They host YouTube AMAs... while ignoring live questions in favor of pre-selected ones.

8) Their BD blocks some of the most valuable community members... while paying influencers who call the project Gigagas L2 instead of RiseX.

9) They keep telling everyone how great the platform is... while there's virtually no liquidity.

10) They build hype around announcements... only for the announcement to be: "Mainnet is currently in maintenance mode."

11) They advertise $10,000 competitions where the top prize is supposed to be $1,500... yet people end up receiving $50.

12) They claim to have a huge active community... while most of the activity comes from moderators.

13) They expect the community to buy NFTs, invest in private rounds, spend hours keeping a dead Discord alive, and publish two posts every week.

Meanwhile the team gets salaries.

The community pays with money.

The community pays with time.

And transparency is treated like an optional feature.

People are skipping one of the easiest opportunities in crypto because they see "$10,000 trading volume" and immediately assume it's expensive or complicated.

It isn't.

@vooi_io  is currently running the @extendedapp  Celebration campaign. The Top 20 traders will split a $7,500 USDC prize pool, while also earning +10% boosted Extended Points, which could become valuable if Extended launches an airdrop in the future.

The funniest part is that there are only 17 qualified participants right now. The campaign pays the Top 20, so if it ended today, every qualified trader would already receive a prize.

Most people think funding arbitrage is difficult because they imagine comparing funding rates across different exchanges, opening positions manually and constantly switching between tabs.

That's not how it works on VOOI.

VOOI already finds the opportunities for you and puts everything in one place. You simply open the Funding Arbitrage page, choose Extended and another supported perp DEX, then open both positions. No spreadsheets, no searching, no jumping between exchanges.

One position is long and the other is short, so they hedge each other. If one side loses money, the other side gains roughly the same amount, which means your market exposure stays close to neutral. The funding difference is where your profit comes from.

I started with just $20 ($10 on Lighter and $10 on Extended) and generated around $40,000 in trading volume. Most of the time I wasn't even looking at the charts - I opened both positions, went to do something else, came back later and closed them.

If you want to try it, it's literally five steps:

1. Open VOOI Ultra - https://t.co/8l9sGlurmS
2. Open the Extended Celebration page and read the rules.
3. Go to the Funding Arbitrage section.
4. Choose Extended and any supported perp DEX.
5. Open the trade and repeat until you reach the required volume.

You're essentially doing three things at once: competing for a share of $7,500, farming potential airdrop points, and potentially earning funding - all with a strategy where your market exposure stays close to zero.

Every new @PythNetwork integration tells the same story.

If a protocol wants to launch a market that never closes, it needs pricing that never stops.

That’s exactly why @dYdX integrated the Pyth 24/7 Oil Index.

24/7 markets only work when the price feed is just as continuous.

One more venue trusting Pyth to keep markets moving.

A developer made $1,000 for one n8n automation. It sent personalized emails with promo codes to a giveaway list. Took a few hours to build.

> This is not a side hustle. This is a repeatable service business. Here's the exact model.

Finding clients is simple. Fill out a contact form on any business website. If the response takes hours or days - that's a broken process you can fix and charge for. Job listings for "data entry" mean manual work that hasn't been automated yet. Both are buying signals.

Don't pitch automation. Build a free basic version first - an email autoresponder, a lead notification to Slack. Show it running. Once a client sees the system working on its own, going back to manual feels impossible. 

- You're not selling scripts. You're selling faster leads and recovered time.

The two templates that sell fastest:

Instant lead responder - client submits a form, they get an acknowledgment email in seconds, owner gets a WhatsApp or Slack notification with full contact details. No more lost leads sitting in an inbox.

Content automation - client drops voice notes or short videos into a folder, system transcribes, rewrites, and splits into ready-to-post social content. Zero editing required.

Why n8n specifically: flat cost. Whether the workflow runs 10 times or 10,000 times, your infrastructure cost doesn't change. More runs = more margin, not more bills. Run it on a VPS - $5/month, 24/7 uptime, instant rollback if something breaks.

Package yourself properly. Build a site, show your automations live. If a prospect fills your contact form and gets an instant response - you've already sold them before the call.

Bookmark this before someone in your city figures it out first.

What would you choose: trading with your own $500, or getting the opportunity to trade with $25,000 by paying just $124?

Sounds like a question with an obvious answer.

And it is.

Let me explain.

@FundingPredicts is a prop firm for prediction markets.

Simply put, if you don't have a large bankroll, the company is willing to provide one. But first, you have to prove that you know how to trade.

There are $25k, $50k, $100k, and $150k accounts. I chose the smallest one - $25,000.

The challenge costs $155, but with the promo code APPLE you get 20% off, bringing the price down to $124. So basically, for $124 you get the opportunity to trade on a $25,000 account.

What's the goal?

You need to make +$1,500 without breaking the drawdown rules. In other words, you only need to make +6% on the starting balance.

Once you complete the challenge, you start trading on a funded account. You keep 80% of the profits, while the company keeps the remaining 20% for providing the capital.

As for me, my balance is currently $26,250.

I've already made +$1,250 out of the required +$1,500. I only need another $250 (just 1% more) to complete the challenge.

And then I'll finally be able to test one theory...

Maybe the problem wasn't that I was a bad trader.

Maybe I just didn't have enough capital.

This honestly feels like one of the most overlooked opportunities in crypto right now.

@vooi_io  just increased the prize pool to $7,500 and extended the campaign for another 7 days.

https://t.co/8l9sGlurmS

After four days, only 16 traders have qualified.

Why?

I think most people see the $10,000 trading volume requirement and immediately assume they need thousands of dollars.

That's not how it works.

VOOI is a perp DEX aggregator. You can open the same market across multiple perp DEXs like Lighter, Hyperliquid, TradeXYZ, Ostium, Extended, and more.

That means you can run funding arbitrage by opening opposite positions on two different venues.

For example:

> Long on Hyperliquid
> Short on Extended

Because the positions offset most of each other's price exposure, you're primarily building leveraged volume rather than taking a directional view on the market.

I started with around $20 split across two platforms.

A few hours later, I'd already generated almost $10,000 in leveraged trading volume.

At the time of writing, I'm actually slightly up, thanks to funding.

And with those exact same trades I'm also:

• Farming Extended Points (+10% through VOOI)
• Earning funding
• Competing for a share of the $7,500 prize pool

The funny part?
I think most people will discover this campaign after it's already over.

I'm backing Australia 🇦🇺 to beat the USA 🇺🇸 today.

Both teams opened the World Cup with convincing wins, making this one of the biggest matches in Group D. The winner takes a huge step toward finishing at the top of the group.

This is also my next trade in the $25,000 funded challenge with @FundingPredicts

The objective is simple: turn a $25,000 funded account into $26,500 while staying within the firm's risk limits.

Unlike most prop firm challenges, nothing here is simulated.

Every position is opened on live Polymarket markets with real liquidity, real news, and real prices. The only difference is that I'm trading with funded capital instead of using only my own bankroll.

Every trade is another step toward the $26,500 target.

Small live example of how funding arbitrage works.

I opened two opposite positions:

> Long $MSTR  on Lighter
> Short $MSTR on Extended

The Lighter leg is slightly negative, while the Extended leg is positive, so the positions are already offsetting each other.

Current uPnL: +0.21%

Funding is still at 0 because the trade was opened only a few minutes ago. If I hold it longer, funding should start kicking in.

At the same time, this trade counts toward the $5,000 Extended Celebration campaign on @vooi_io 

Price-neutral exposure, Extended Points farming, leaderboard volume, and campaign participation - all in one setup.

$5,000 is up for grabs, and you don’t need to predict where ETH or BTC goes next.

One strategy lets you farm Extended Points, climb the leaderboard, and compete for a share of the prize pool at the same time.

It’s called funding arbitrage.

Instead of trying to predict the next market move, you open opposite positions on two different perp DEXs.

For example:

> Long ETH on Lighter.
> Short ETH on Extended.

If ETH moves in either direction, one position largely offsets the other. The objective is to capture funding rate differences while building leveraged trading volume.

For the Extended Celebration campaign, every funding arbitrage trade that includes Extended counts toward the leaderboard, while Extended volume receives a 2x boost for ranking.

On top of that, every new wallet registered through @vooi_io gets a 10% boost to Extended Points, so you’re farming Extended Points while competing for a share of the $5,000 USDC prize pool.

Getting started is simple:

1. Open VOOI : https://t.co/8l9sGlurmS
2. Open the Extended Celebration campaign page and read the rules.
3. Go to Funding Arbitrage.
4. Select @extendedapp plus any other supported perp DEX.
5. Choose your asset and leverage.
6. Open the trade.

You only need $10,000 in leveraged trading volume to qualify, and thanks to leverage, that can be reached with a relatively small amount of capital.

Looks like a solid opportunity if you’re already farming points - or planning to start

Traditional stock exchanges close in the evening and spend the next 16 hours asleep, leaving behind massive price gaps.

Meanwhile, SpaceX stock now trades 24 hours a day, 7 days a week - and @PythNetwork  is making that possible.

SpaceX just completed the largest IPO in history, raising $75 billion at a price of $135 per share.

The stock opened at $150 and surged above $200 within just a few trading sessions.

Its market capitalization now exceeds $2.5 trillion.

Elon Musk has officially become the first trillionaire on the planet.

But while the world is busy debating valuations and rockets, something far more important happened for crypto.

On the first day of trading, Pyth Network launched a full $SPCX  price feed.

Premarket, regular trading hours, postmarket, and overnight sessions are all delivered through a single continuous stream of data.

And quite recently, Pyth took another step forward by expanding support for some markets from 24/5 to 24/7.

Pyth is more than just an oracle.

It now provides over 3,000 price feeds sourced from more than 100 institutional data providers pushing prices directly into the network.

They gather information from real market makers and major trading firms, then deliver it onchain in real time.

While Wall Street turns off the lights and heads home, crypto can continue trading shares of one of the world's most valuable companies without interruption.

Perpetual exchanges, DeFi protocols, and onchain derivatives gain access to the same quality of data used by institutions - only it's available around the clock.

This isn't crypto trying to catch up.

It's a structural advantage.

The old financial system was built around limited trading hours.

The new world that Pyth is helping build no longer has that limitation.

Pyth is becoming the infrastructure layer that genuinely connects traditional finance and crypto.

And every future giant that goes public will be expected to exist in both worlds from day one.

Billions of dollars are traded every weekend across perps, prediction markets, and tokenized assets.

The funny part?

Most of the pricing infrastructure behind these markets was never designed for a world that trades 24/7.

If you think about it, it's actually pretty strange.

Crypto never sleeps.

Prediction markets never sleep.

More and more assets are becoming available around the clock.

Yet many of the benchmarks and pricing systems they rely on still come from traditional markets built around opening bells, closing bells, weekends, and holidays.

That's the gap @PythNetwork  is trying to close.

Pyth just launched Pyth Indices, a new suite of 24/7 indices covering oil, metals, U.S. equities, and thematic baskets like AI10, Defense10, China10, and Tech100.

The goal is simple: create pricing infrastructure that matches the reality of modern markets.

And this isn't some roadmap or future vision.

Coinbase has already launched thematic equity index futures powered by Pyth and MarketVector.

Kraken is already using Pyth Indices for oil derivatives.

dYdX already offers a perpetual market based on Pyth's 24/7 Oil Index.

Nado uses continuous oil pricing across spot, margin, and perpetual products.

The thematic baskets were also co-developed with MarketVector, VanEck's index business, whose benchmarks are tracked by more than $100B in investment products.

We've spent years building 24/7 markets.

Now we're building the infrastructure they actually need.

Imagine paying less than the size of a single @Polymarket  position...

Prediction markets have produced some of the sharpest traders in crypto.
